goal: I want a little program I can run from a command window to keep track of what I spend. First it needs somewhere to save my expenses so they are still there the next time I open it. Then I want to be able to type in an expense — how much it cost, what kind of thing it was (like groceries or fuel), and the day I bought it. And I want to be able to ask it for a list of everything I have spent so far, newest first, so I can look back over it.

tasks.prompt: Build the persistence layer for a command-line expense tracker in this repo: a Python module src/expenses/storage.py that saves and loads expense records to a JSON-lines file on disk so data survives between runs. Provide init_store(path) (creates the file and parent dirs if missing), append_expense(store_path, expense), and read_expenses(store_path) returning a list of expense dicts. An expense is {"amount": float, "category": str, "date_iso": str}. Include unit tests.

contract.notes: expense = {amount: float, category: str, date_iso: 'YYYY-MM-DD'}. JSON-lines file storage; init_store creates parent dirs; read_expenses returns [] for a missing/empty store.

tasks.prompt: Add the 'add' command to the expense tracker CLI: src/expenses/cli_add.py exposing add_expense_command(argv) wired so `python -m expenses add --amount 12.50 --category groceries --date 2026-07-01` persists one expense via the existing storage module. Validate amount is a positive number and date is YYYY-MM-DD; print a one-line confirmation; exit non-zero with a clear message on bad input. Include unit tests.

contract.notes: Persists via storage-module's append_expense; rejects amount <= 0 and non-ISO dates with exit code 2; prints 'added <amount> <category> <date>' on success.

tasks.prompt: Add the 'list' command to the expense tracker CLI: src/expenses/cli_list.py exposing list_expenses_command(argv) wired so `python -m expenses list` prints every stored expense newest-first, one per line as '<date>  <amount>  <category>', using the existing storage module to read them. An empty store prints 'no expenses yet' and exits 0. Include unit tests covering ordering and the empty case.

contract.notes: Reads via storage-module's read_expenses; sorts newest-first by date_iso; empty store prints 'no expenses yet', exit 0.

criteria.text: the project's code compiles and its test suite passes on the integrated tree
criteria.check: pytest exits 0 at the repo root

criteria.text: an added expense is persisted and appears in the list output
criteria.check: add 12.50 groceries 2026-07-01, then list shows a line with 2026-07-01, 12.50 and groceries
a second process running list still shows the previously added expense
add two expenses with different dates; the later date is printed first
